checks.name: rate-accuracy
checks.details: Universal 5-tier acceleration multipliers (1×/1.25×/1.5×/2×/2.25×) and tier boundaries confirmed against AE, ISR, SDR, SE, and Channel Calculator formulas (e.g., AE!G15–G19, SE!G11–G15). Retention floor formula (2×TargetRate−1) confirmed in AM!G8, CSM!G8, RM!G8, AEAM!G10. Payout interpolation (0.5+(actual−floor)/(1−floor)) and 1.5 cap at actual≥1 confirmed in AM!G9, CSM!G9, RM!G9, AEAM!G11. payout_at_target=1.0 mathematically verified: at actual=target → 0.5+(target−(2t−1))/(1−(2t−1)) = 0.5+0.5 = 1.0. Quarterly draw rates confirmed: CSM!G17=G6/4*0.75 (75%), AM!G18=G6/4*0.5 (50%), RM!G17=G6/4*0.75. ISR 60/40 quarterly split confirmed via ISR!G6=(C6/4)*0.6 and G7=(C6/4)*0.4. AEAM 80/20 non-OTT and 60/40 OTT confirmed via AEAM!G6:G7. AE renewal multi-year flat rate (0.50%) and ISR pass-up rates ($50 flat, 2% CW) cannot be confirmed — source formulas G27, G42–G43 fall outside the provided formula window.

checks.name: quota-consistency
checks.details: No quota dollar amounts are hardcoded in extracted rules; all quota values are treated as employee-level inputs consistent with CIQ Employee Assumptions. Weight splits are internally consistent across Plan Summary rows and confirmed calculator formulas. ISR/SDR Manager (0.25+0.25+0.50=1.0) and CSM/SE Manager (0.75+0.125+0.125=1.0) are arithmetically correct. No cross-rule quota mismatches detected.

checks.name: completeness
checks.details: ISR/SDR Manager and CSM/SE Manager calculator tabs are absent from the workbook; both manager role splits rely solely on Plan Summary inference. The Plan Summary exposes only two weight columns (Comp 1% / Comp 2%), leaving the third component weight for both manager roles unverifiable from source data. Additionally, CIQ Employee Assumptions omit the following fields required by extracted rules: multi-year deal incremental ARR by duration (24mo/36mo/48mo/60mo+), Renewal Multi-Year Previous ARR, passed-opportunity count, closed-deal value for pass-up closed-won, and current-quarter tracking for draw logic. These gaps will block formula execution for ae-multi-year-acceleration, ae-renewal-multi-year-flat-rate, isr-passup-flat-spif, isr-passup-closed-won-spif, and all quarterly draw rules in CIQ before a build can complete.

checks.name: contradictions
checks.details: Two rule-level contradictions identified. (1) multi-year-bcr-qualifier lists AM as an applicable role, but ae-multi-year-acceleration excludes AM. AM Calculator contains no multi-year deal input rows, and AM's plan structure (Expansion + Retention only, no NNB component) has no duration-based bonus mechanism — the qualifier can never trigger a downstream rule for AM. (2) services-bookings-half-bcr sets applicable_roles to 'All' while services-exclusion-qualifier scopes the exclusion to only AE, AEAM Hybrid, and SE. This creates ambiguity for ISR, RM, and CSM: it is unclear whether the 50% BCR modifier applies to these roles when their calculator tabs contain no services booking rows, risking phantom commission generation.

checks.name: formula-feasibility
checks.details: Standard rate-table, split, and retention draw formulas reference fields present in CIQ Employee Assumptions (Annual OTC, role-specific quotas, Target Retention Rate, Segment, Region, BCR Proration Method) and are feasible to build. However, multi-year deal bonus formulas require per-deal incremental ARR inputs by duration tier not present in Employee Assumptions or any listed Data Worksheet. ISR pass-up SPIF formulas require passed-opportunity count and pass-up closed deal value, also absent. Q4 true-up draw logic requires current-quarter tracking present in calculator tabs as a cell input but not included in CIQ assumptions. These must be added as transaction-level actuals feeds or supplemental input worksheets before CIQ configuration begins.

flaggedRules.reason: AM is listed in applicable_roles but AM has no NNB quota, no multi-year deal input rows in the AM Calculator, and is explicitly absent from ae-multi-year-acceleration. AM's plan structure (Expansion + Retention only) provides no pathway to trigger a duration-based deal bonus. Including AM creates dead qualifier logic that would silently fail in CIQ with no accelerator to call.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Remove 'AM' from applicable_roles. Correct scope is AE, AEAM Hybrid, ISR (ISR pending verification per separate flag). If AM is intended to receive a multi-year expansion bonus, extract it as a distinct rule with its own accelerator definition.

flaggedRules.reason: ISR is listed as applicable but the Instructions text explicitly states 'AEs: Multi-Year incentives use duration-based BCR multipliers (1.2x–2x)' with no mention of ISR. No ISR multi-year deal input rows are visible in the provided ISR Calculator data or formula window. ISR inclusion is consistent with the CIQ component list but is not confirmed from calculator source.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Review ISR Calculator rows beyond the visible formula window (G28+) to confirm presence of 24mo/36mo/48mo/60mo+ deal ARR input rows. If absent, remove ISR from applicable_roles and from the CIQ Multi-Year Deal Bonus component. If present, document the source cell reference to close the validation gap.

flaggedRules.reason: applicable_roles is set to 'All' but RM and CSM calculator tabs contain no services booking input rows. Applying a 50% BCR modifier to roles with no services booking metric would generate phantom commission. The rule description explicitly calls out Channel dual-BCR behavior, which is not captured by the 'All' shorthand and requires explicit enumeration.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Restrict applicable_roles to roles confirmed to have services booking inputs: AE, AEAM Hybrid, ISR, SE, Channel. Align scope with services-exclusion-qualifier and enumerate Channel explicitly to capture the dual-BCR variant. Remove RM and CSM unless their plans are updated to include services booking rows.

flaggedRules.reason: Comp 2 component name is 'Net New Bookings' in params but the SDR Calculator header explicitly labels it 'C2: Regional Net New Bookings (20%)' and the Instructions FY26 Key Changes entry reads 'SDRs: 80% SQOs / 20% Regional NNB'. The 'Regional' qualifier controls crediting territory attribution and must appear in the CIQ component name to correctly scope bookings to the rep's assigned region.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Rename params.components[1].name from 'Net New Bookings' to 'Regional Net New Bookings'. Ensure the corresponding CIQ component definition and Regional Territory Map attribute worksheet use this exact label for consistent crediting logic.

flaggedRules.reason: Plan Summary exposes only Comp 1% = 0.25 and Comp 2% = 0.25. The third component (SQOs at 50%) is not present as a distinct column and was inferred from context. No ISR/SDR Manager calculator tab exists to validate component weights, confirm quarterly timing, or verify that the SQO component is a rollup of subordinate SDR attainment versus a direct quota.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Obtain an ISR/SDR Manager calculator tab or written plan confirmation before deploying this rule in CIQ. Tag as provisional in CIQ and apply a manual approval gate. Clarify whether SQO weight is based on SDR-team rollup quota or an independent manager quota, as this determines which CIQ hierarchy linkage to configure.

flaggedRules.reason: Plan Summary shows Comp 1% = 0.75 and Comp 2% = 0.125 but has no Comp 3% column to confirm the 12.5% Regional Bookings weight. No CSM/SE Manager calculator tab exists. The three-way split is arithmetically consistent (0.75+0.125+0.125=1.0) but the Regional Bookings third component is sourced entirely from inference with no cell-level reference.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Obtain CSM/SE Manager calculator tab or written plan documentation before deploying. Confirm whether the 12.5% Expansion component uses CSM-team rollup or regional quota, and whether the 12.5% Regional Bookings references SE-team bookings or a combined CSM/SE regional figure. These distinctions require different CIQ hierarchy configurations.

flaggedRules.reason: The 0.50% flat rate is attributed to AE Calculator!G27, which falls outside the provided formula window (visible formulas end near G22 with 15 additional formulas unlisted). The rate cannot be confirmed or denied from available data. AEAM Hybrid applicability is also asserted without a corresponding formula cell reference.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Expose AE Calculator formula G27 and the analogous AEAM Hybrid Calculator formula to confirm the 0.50% rate and basis. If AEAM Hybrid uses a different rate or a different prior-ARR definition, split this into two separate rules to avoid a single rule governing two potentially divergent implementations.

validatedRules.description: Universal 5-tier acceleration table: 1× (0–100%), 1.25× (100–125%), 1.5× (125–150%), 2× (150–200%), 2.25× (200%+) applied to all roles' tiered components.
Retention scaled payout: floor = 2×TargetRate−1; below floor → 0%; at floor → 50%; at 100% → 150% cap; linear interpolation between thresholds.
Multi-year deal bonus: incremental ARR × BCR × duration multiplier (1.2× for 24mo, 1.35× for 36mo, 1.5× for 48mo, 2× for 60mo+).
AE/AEAM Hybrid renewal multi-year deal bonus at flat 0.50% of prior ARR.
Services bookings earn 50% of the role's primary BCR; Channel roles earn 50% of both Sourced and Fulfilled BCRs.
AE (Named/Ent/MM, SMB-Media): 100% Net New Bookings, annual timing.
AEAM Hybrid: non-OTT 80/20 NNB+Expansion/Retention; OTT 60/40 NNB+Expansion/Retention; annual timing.
AM: non-OTT 80/20 Expansion/Retention; OTT 60/40 Expansion/Retention; annual timing.
ISR: 60% Closed Won Opps / 40% Net New Bookings, quarterly timing.
SDR: 80% SQOs (quarterly) / 20% Regional NNB (annual).
RM: 80% Retention / 20% Expansion, annual timing.
CSM: 80% Retention / 20% Expansion, annual timing, regional crediting.
Channel: Sourced/Fulfilled weights vary by segment — Strategic 100/0, R&D 40/60, Regional-60/40, Regional-50/50.
SE: 100% Regional Bookings, annual timing.
ISR/SDR Manager: 25% Closed Won (ISR rollup), 25% NNB (ISR rollup), 50% SQOs (SDR rollup), quarterly.
CSM/SE Manager: 75% Retention (CSM rollup), 12.5% Expansion (regional), 12.5% Regional Bookings (regional), annual.
CSM/RM retention draw: 75% of quarterly retention variable in Q1–Q3; Q4 annual true-up.
AEAM Hybrid/AM retention draw: 50% of quarterly retention variable in Q1–Q3; Q4 annual true-up.
January 2026: flat draw = 1/12 annual vOTE for all roles; February offline estimated commission; March true-up required.
ISR earns $50 flat per opportunity passed to an AE, regardless of outcome.
ISR earns 2% of closed deal value on passed-up opportunities that close won.
Services bookings are excluded from NNB quota credit; paid separately at 50% BCR.

parameters.condition: Services bookings excluded from NNB credit

validatedRules.description: Multi-year deal bonus requires contract duration ≥24 months to qualify for duration-based BCR multiplier.
Channel credit assignment changed from rep-based to region-based for FY26.
CSM crediting changed from rep-based to regional for FY26.
Mid-period goal changes must support fixed or prorated BCR/quota adjustment options.
Retention component payout capped at 150% of retention variable when actual retention ≥ 100%.
Retention payout drops to 0% below the floor rate (2×TargetRate−1); acts as a minimum threshold gate.

payoutComponents: NNB Commission (AE, AEAM, ISR, SDR, SE, Channel)
Retention Quarterly Draw (AM, AEAM, RM, CSM)
Manager Rollup Payout (ISR/SDR Mgr, CSM/SE Mgr)
planStructure.notes: Monthly periods aggregate into quarterly (ISR, SDR) and annual (all other roles) measurement windows. Retention draws pay Q1–Q3 with Q4 true-up. January 2026 is a flat draw month requiring March true-up. BCR proration toggle (fixed vs prorated) needed for mid-period goal changes.

dataWorksheets.name: Universal Acceleration Tiers
dataWorksheets.description: 5-tier commission rate multiplier schedule applied to all tiered components.

dataWorksheets.name: Multi-Year Deal Multipliers
dataWorksheets.description: Duration-based BCR multipliers for multi-year contracts ≥24 months.

dataWorksheets.name: Retention Payout Schedule
dataWorksheets.description: Scaled retention payout curve: floor→50%, target→100%, ceiling→150%.

dataWorksheets.description: Defines component weights per role/segment combination for OTC allocation.

dataWorksheets.description: ISR pass-up incentive rates: flat per-opp and percentage on closed-won.

dataWorksheets.name: Retention Draw Rates
dataWorksheets.description: Quarterly retention draw rates by role group.

attributeWorksheets.name: Role-Segment Assignment
attributeWorksheets.description: Maps reps to role, segment, and component weights.

attributeWorksheets.name: Regional Territory Map
attributeWorksheets.description: Maps accounts to regions for CSM, Channel, SE, SDR crediting.

attributeWorksheets.name: Manager Rollup Hierarchy
attributeWorksheets.description: Manager-to-rep rollup for ISR/SDR and CSM/SE Manager plans.

formulaRecommendations.description: Tiered commission using universal acceleration table.
formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Calculate attainment, determine BCR, apply marginal rate multiplier per tier on incremental bookings within each band.
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: SmartGrid with TIER_LOOKUP referencing Universal Acceleration Tiers. MARGINAL tier mode. BCR from Employee Assumptions.

formulaRecommendations.description: Retention scaled payout with floor and cap.
formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Compute floor = 2×TargetRate−1. Below floor: 0%. At/above 100%: 150%. Otherwise interpolate linearly.
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Custom SmartGrid formula (continuous curve, not discrete tiers). Helper column for Floor. Reference Target Retention Rate from Assumptions.

formulaRecommendations.description: Quarterly retention draw with Q4 true-up.
formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Q1–Q3: pay DrawRate × (RetentionVariable/4). Q4: MAX(0, AnnualRetentionPayout − 3×QuarterlyDraw).
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Period Group logic for Q1–Q3 vs Q4. DrawRate from Retention Draw Rates worksheet. Track cumulative draws in running-total column.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: For deals ≥24 months: bonus = IncrementalARR × BCR × DurationMultiplier. Renewal MY: PriorARR × 0.5%.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula:
IF DealDuration >= 24 THEN Multiplier = LOOKUP(DealDuration, MultiYearTable); Bonus = ARR * BCR * Multiplier;
RenewalMYBonus = PriorARR * 0.005;
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: VLOOKUP against Multi-Year Deal Multipliers. Deal-level transaction field for DealDuration required. Separate payout line for renewal MY.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Services excluded from NNB quota credit, earn 50% of primary BCR. Channel: 50% of both Sourced and Fulfilled BCRs.
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Separate payout line filtered by booking_type='Services'. Channel needs two sub-lines for Sourced/Fulfilled services.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: $50 per passed opp + 2% of closed-won deal value on passed opps.
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Requires Passed Opportunities transaction source with PassedDate, DealValue, ClosedWonFlag. Two payout lines.

formulaRecommendations.description: January 2026 flat draw and March true-up.
formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: January = vOTE/12 flat draw. February offline. March true-up reconciles Jan+Feb against actual.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula:
JanuaryPayout = AnnualVOTE / 12;
MarchTrueUp = ActualQ1Commission - JanuaryPayout - FebruaryEstimate;
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: January draw as manual adjustment. February offline tracked as prior-period adjustment. March references both. Use Prior Period Adjustments column.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: ISR/SDR Mgr: sum direct report metrics. CSM/SE Mgr: regional aggregation (not sum-of-reps).
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula:
ISRSDRMgr: MgrMetrics = SUM(DirectReports.Metrics);
CSMSEMgr: MgrRetention = RegionalRate; MgrExpansion = RegionalTotal;
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: CaptivateIQ hierarchical rollup via Manager Rollup Hierarchy. CSM/SE Mgr uses region-level aggregation. Manager quotas set independently in Assumptions.

insights: Extraction quality is mechanically strong — the core compensation structures (universal acceleration tiers, retention floor/cap/interpolation model, quarterly draw rates, comp weight splits) are accurately captured and formula-verified against multiple calculator tabs with no rate or threshold errors detected. All three corrections are scoped and low-complexity. The most critical production risk is the CIQ configuration gap: multi-year deal ARR inputs, renewal prior-ARR, ISR pass-up transaction data, and current-quarter tracking are all required by active formula rules but are entirely absent from the Employee Assumptions schema — this will cause build failures before any testing occurs and must be remediated before CIQ configuration begins. Manager-role rules (ISR/SDR and CSM/SE) remain the highest-risk extractions due to absent calculator tabs and single-source Plan Summary inference; both should be gated as provisional and withheld from production deployment until verified. Seven rules have elements that cannot be confirmed due to formula rows falling outside the provided data window; a complete workbook export covering all formula rows is strongly recommended before the extraction is considered closed.
